feedback.text: I appreciate the intent behind the Digital Fairness Act and its efforts to uphold consumer rights in the digital space. I would like to propose an addition to further enhance consumer protections. Specifically for the video game industry (Stop Destroying Videogames). Suggested Addition: Introduce provisions that prevent publishers or developers from destroying a video game after it has been purchased. Why It Matters: Consumers often buy video games with the expectation of long-term access to their video games. However, some games have been made unaccessable post-purchase. This practice undermines ownership rights and erodes consumer trust. Adding this protection would help ensure that digital products, especially games, remain usable and fair to consumers after the point of sale, just like physical goods. Thank you for considering this addition to the Act. I believe it would close a growing gap in consumer rights for digital products.
